title: WordPress Security Updates
description:
Hi Predrag, please action the following for johnston-lawfirm.com:


User access changes: No changes requested — all three listed users (Brad, Marc, Andrew) confirmed to remain as-is.


2FA: Approved — client requests users be prompted to set up on their own rather than being auto-locked out. Note: Brad will notify Marc's admin separately to handle Marc's 2FA setup.


Secure login URL: Approved — client has one request: please do a quick before-and-after performance check (load time / Core Web Vitals) to confirm no impact to site speed or SEO.
johnston-lawfirm.com/backstage


Please also add me as an Administrator on the site (if you're not already). — Jessica Mena | jmena@clixsy.com

Thank you!

title: GSC Indexing Error — Pages Blocked Due to 4xx Issue
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Keith Bruss, Mubeen Dildar-Mia, Predrag
description:
Google Search Console sent an alert on May 17, 2026 indicating that pages on johnston-lawfirm.com are being prevented from indexing due to a new reason: "Blocked due to other 4xx issue." Brad forwarded this to Jess as an FYI, noting he assumes Clixsy receives these alerts as well. The affected pages and full scope of the issue need to be identified via the GSC indexing report and resolved.

Client Goal: Identify all pages affected by the 4xx indexing block and resolve the issue so affected pages can be crawled and indexed by Google.

Requirements for Completion:
  • GSC indexing report is reviewed to identify all URLs affected by the "Blocked due to other 4xx issue" error
  • Root cause of the 4xx block is identified
  • Affected pages are fixed so they are no longer blocked from indexing
  • GSC indexing report is confirmed to show resolution following fixes
  • AM is notified once resolved so Brad can be updated
 Image and image text:


 

"New reason preventing your pages from being indexed 
Search Console has identified that some pages on your site are not being indexed due to the following new reason:
Blocked due to other 4xx issue 
If this reason is not intentional, we recommend that you fix it in order to get affected pages indexed and appearing on Google.
Open indexing report 
 
Message type: [WNC-20237597] 
You've received this transactional email because your site is listed in Google Search Console. You may  unsubscribe from this type of message or  add partners who should receive messages for this Search Console account 
Google LLC, 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway Mountain View, CA 94043 
"

__________________________________________________________________________________________

  I'm posting this here, from the new Task Priority SOP, for your reference, since we have started the new Priority Ticketing system.

Thanks!

title: Add Sports Partnership to Community Page
description:
Add section to community page to highlight their sports partnership
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/community/
Structure to use:
Title: Johnston Law Firm:
Proud Portland Trail Blazers Small Business Partner

Date/Subtext:
🏀 Exciting News

Image:

Text: 

Johnston Law Firm is proud to be a Small Business Partner of the Portland Trail Blazers. This partnership is about more than basketball. It reflects our commitment to Oregon, the people who call it home, and the responsibility we feel to show up for our community. The Trail Blazers are woven into the fabric of this state, and we’re grateful to support something that brings Oregonians together.
As a personal injury law firm, we stand up for people when they need it most. That same commitment extends beyond our work and into the community we serve.

Contact Us for a FREE Consultation
If you have been seriously injured without fault of your own, please contact us using the form below.

cc

title: LSA Report – Pull & Send to Brad Alban
description:
Hey David,

Brad reached out, concerned about Google rankings after their principal, Marc, flagged that Johnston Law Firm is no longer appearing in the top Google Ads sponsored section. Corey and Jessica have already addressed the broader rankings conversation. The outstanding action item is for the team to pull an LSA report for Brad and set up a recurring send.


Action Items:

  •  Pull the LSA report for Johnston Law Firm. ( Can you please let me know when you'll be available to send you the LSA verification to sign into the dashboard?)
  •  Send report to Brad Alban
  •      can you see if the LSA Google rep can pull a market report (per Corey's suggestion)


https://clixsy-help.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/4302

title: Remove 21 Million Average Case Stat from Homepage — URGENT
description:

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/

Summary: The client has flagged a "21 Million Average Case" statistic currently displayed on the homepage as urgent. Marc has seen it, and the firm wants it removed immediately due to Bar compliance concerns about suggesting an average-case result. The client has provided a rough concept for what could replace it — an icon linking to their case study pages — but has indicated this is not prescriptive and is open to options.

Client Goal: Remove the 21 Million Average Case statistic from the homepage immediately and replace it with something that drives visitors toward case results without implying an average outcome.

Requirements for Completion:
  • "21 Million Average Case" statistic is removed from the homepage
  • Replacement element is added in its place, incorporating:
    • An icon
    • A link to the firm's case study pages
    • Suggested language: "Trusted" or "Case Results" with supporting copy such as "See some of the work we have done with clients"
  • Replacement element is consistent in formatting and styling with surrounding homepage sections
  • No language on the homepage implies or suggests an average case result
Deadline: Urgent — client explicitly flagged this. Marc has already seen the stat. Move as quickly as possible. 

---
Thanks much!

title: Checking if SMPT implementation is good to move forward with
description:
The Johnston team is using Gravity Form for their marketing/community page, and while submissions are being captured correctly, the notification emails don’t appear to be coming through reliably. 
 
Brad would like to implement SMTP (via Post SMTP) on our WordPress instance to improve deliverability and make sure notifications are consistently received.
 
The plan would be to route outgoing mail through our Microsoft 365 SMTP (smtp.office365.com) using authenticated sending and a consistent “From” address (welcome@johnston-lawfirm.com), and to enforce that at the application level to avoid any plugin or theme overrides.
 
Before moving forward, Brad would like to double-check this aligns with anything already in place or any preferences you have around email handling.

title: Updated SEO scope for revised proposal
description:
SEO

GBP promotion: 

- Foundational offsite promotion (citations, profiles, 2nd tier links)
- Supplementary offsite promotion (local surge / property stacks

- Proprietary user journey signals and entity promotion (e.g. image rankings, embeds, supporting URL's / pages purely for GBP coverage expansion)

Digital PR and link building

- Campaigns: 1 new campaign initiated Quarterly (but will last until quota hit) 12 target links quota (typically results in 30 to 40 including syndicated links.

Campaign schedule
Weeks 1-3: Creative Ideation & Idea Viability Check (completed)
Weeks 3-5: Data Collection and Story Production
Weeks 5-8: Outreach and Pitching

Onsite SEO
- Onpage optimizations
- site structure changes or adaptations
- Internal linking updates
- Content provisions or changes (targeted, not random blogging)
- AI analysis recommendations fulfilled (content gaps, content focus, Key seo zones, internal link equity, on-site modifications that come out of ongoing R&D

title: Car accident lawyer keyword ranking
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Clayton Coombs, Corey V
description:
Discussion: How can we gain on car accident lawyer keyword - , please review Rob Klein's website and the strategy Mockingbird is employing. Rob Klein is an attorney who also works with them but they don't want to see his website being able to pull leads the firm should have. 

Here are the Ai notes from the discussion Corey had w/ Bill about car accident lawyer: 
    • Johnston Law average rank 7.8 for car accident terms
      • Johnston Law's average rank of 7.8 for car accident terms indicates a competitive presence in the market.
        This ranking suggests that the firm is effectively optimizing its content and SEO strategies to attract potential clients searching for car accident legal services.
      • Improving this rank further could lead to increased visibility and a higher volume of inquiries for car accident cases.
      • Rob Klein's website unexpectedly outperforming

      • Rob Klein's website has a high domain authority due to its long-standing presence and relevance in the cycling community.

      • The website's SEO strategy includes keyword stuffing in titles, which boosts its visibility in search rankings.

      • There is concern about the website attracting potential clients away from Johnston Law Firm, as Rob Klein receives a percentage from cases originated through his site.

      • Keyword stuffing in titles contributing to Rob's performance
        • Keyword stuffing in titles can lead to improved search engine rankings by increasing relevance for specific search terms.
        • This practice can enhance visibility in search results, attracting more clicks and traffic to the website.
        • However, excessive keyword stuffing may negatively impact user experience and lead to penalties from search engines if deemed manipulative.
        • Need to build more authority around car accident terms
        • Building authority around car accident terms can be achieved through the following strategies:
          Create high-quality, informative content that addresses common questions and concerns related to car accidents, such as legal rights, insurance claims, and safety tips.
          Engage in local SEO practices by optimizing website content for relevant keywords, ensuring accurate business listings, and gathering positive reviews from clients.
          Establish backlinks from reputable sources, such as legal blogs, news articles, and local business directories, to enhance credibility and improve search engine rankings.

title: Request from Marc
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Clayton Coombs, Corey V
description:
Do you guys think the idea below is good or is there an alternative route for the client to take to accomplish their goal of rankings for accidents involving big corporation contractors:

Marc wants to put resources towards building our ranking on SERPs for keywords related to accidents involving companies like Amazon delivery services, DoorDash, Uber, Lyft, GrubHub, Instacart etc.
 
They don't want to take away from the efforts to rank for the big keywords like “Car accident attorney” but want to build out what they need to compete for this perceived niche area.
 
Bill suggested that Marc should write an article with his opinion on who is responsible for these companies’ contractors injuring folks and his theory on how to approach to hold the big corporations accountable.

title: CopyCat Notice
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Clayton Coombs, Corey V, Fed
description:
Hi guys! 

Johnston Law Firm received a copycat notification and asked us: 

Hi Team,
 
Wondering if you all have the proof of getting the referenced image from Shutterstock or similar so we can make these folks go away.

I believe this was around the time of our transition away from Nifty to Clixsy so I am not sure which agency posted the referenced page/photo.
 
Also, this could be a total scam, and I am just not sure.   Any help you could provide will be appreciated.
 
Thanks!
*Link to the email in basecamp if you rather read it that way. *

Sent via email to marc@johnston-lawfirm.com on September 25, 2024
Authorization request / Unauthorized image use - Case no: C86EC3
Dear Johnston Law Firm, P.C.,
We, COPYTRACK, are writing to you on behalf of our client YayImages, who has assigned us the monitoring and protection of their licenses and image rights globally. On September 24, 2024 we have been informed that Johnston Law Firm, P.C. is likely using an image without permission and the client has exclusively commissioned us with the clarification, administration of the image rights and, if necessary, the enforcement of any copyright infringement through our partner lawyers. Images are protected by copyright law and infringements are actionable under international law and the respective national law. Please see the attachment below for details.
On behalf of our client, we must first determine if you have a valid license to use the images in question. If you have a valid license or any other legal justification to use these images, please reply to this email and include proof of license purchase and/or any other necessary information to validate the usage.

Please note that you are obliged to provide the necessary information about the usage of the image to allow us to verify the lawfulness of such usage.

Please respond to this letter no later than October 5, 2024.

If this is not the case, your use of the image material most likely constitutes a copyright infringement. If no settlement can be reached, we as Copytrack are directly instructed to enforce these rights for the territory in the Federal Republic of Germany through our German partner lawyers and, in addition, to instruct our respective partner lawyers in other countries to examine and, if necessary, enforce the infringement of the rights in the respective countries. You would then be legally obligated to compensate our client for the damage caused by this copyright infringement.
Rights holder | YayImages
Image found on | https://johnston-lawfirm.com/how-to-have-fun-and-play-it-safe-during-portlands-world-naked-bike-ride/
Online Since | December 21, 2023
3 steps to solving your caseIn order to resolve this case amicably with you, we request your cooperation. Please complete the following steps to close this case:1.   CheckReview the evidence on this case by going to https://portal.copytrack.com and entering CASE ID C86EC32.   ProofShow us proof of your license by uploading it or providing any other legal justification to use these images and if it is valid, we will close your case immediately;If you do not have a valid license or any other valid justification:3.   Compensation / LicenseYou now have the following 2 options:o  Option 1: Purchase of a subsequent image license (includes past and future use for one year after purchase).o  Option 2: Compensation (includes the previous use and requires the immediate deletion of the image).
Image license (valid for 1 year from date of purchase) | 978.09 €
Compensation costs (past usage) | 900.00 €
Payment is due by | October 24, 2024Payments for both options can be made securely online at https://portal.copytrack.com by entering CASE ID C86EC3.

We accept PayPal, credit card and bank transfer.

We calculate these fees based on our client's license history, as well as the duration of use and type of rights infringement. Failure to provide a proof or pay for a valid license may result in your case being referred to our partner attorney and significantly higher additional fees.

To avoid further action, including legal action, provide proof of a valid license or any other relevant information by October 5, 2024, or acquire it by making payment in our online portal.
Sincerely yours,
Marcus Schmitt
(CEO)
About CopytrackSince 2015, Copytrack has been helping creatives and brands protect their copyrighted images. As one of the leading digital licensing and copyright agents in the world, we have helped manage, license, and enforce over 200.000 cases of image theft. Though most cases settle peacefully, we reserve the right to proceed legally where necessary through our global network of partner law firms in order to protect the intellectual property of our customers.
Summary - Unauthorized use of the image – Case ID: C86EC3For the full report, visit https://portal.copytrack.com and enter this CASE-ID C86EC3.

title: Update Theme to PHP 8.2
description:
Look into what it will take to update the theme to support PHP 8.2

https://johnston-lawfirm.clixsysites.com/

We want to start with an estimate of time.  I know that can be very hard without actually jumping into the project.

If the hours is excessive we may want to bring this up with the client manager.  We may want to make a recommendation for an alternative solution.

title: Question from Mark/ fed to update team
description:
Bill emailed in a question from Mark and I'd like some help in answering this, as I'm not sure if Corey's had a chance to review it: 

  1. On the homepage, when you hover over the “locations” tab it lists 6 cities.  That could be very misleading to potential clients from throughout Oregon.  And of course, we serve the entire state.  At the bare minimum, when we hover over the locations tab there needs to be a tab that says “Oregon” or something signifying that we serve more than those 6 cities.

How do you guys want me to respond to Bill which in turn would update Mark.

title: Johnston Law: Monthly Reporting Call Prep
description:
How did April shape up for leads?

LSA performance details for Johnston Law Firm (CID: 864-159-8917) since April:

- Impressions: 4,075
- Total Leads: 91
- Phone Responsiveness: 85% (L90D)


BrightLocal Reports:
  • Portland
Notes from Andrei

Analytics Summary

https://docs.google.com/document/d/19KmnagEAHxbARmdiSoWbAHLHtGxiqi9rTclpQjb0x6s/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.jq1qcgwhzod7 

The statistics in AHREFs look unfavorable, but this seems to be a common trend for nearly every website I’ve checked with AHREFs in the past few months. However, Google Search Console (GSC) and Bright Local data appear acceptable. On the other hand, GA4 is showing a decline in traffic acquisition over the past 30 days compared to the previous 30 days, likely due to issues with the Google Business Profile (GBP), as detailed below. Thus, the GA4 data presented in the report reflects only the traffic acquisition difference from one year ago.

Additionally, it seems there is an ongoing issue with the GBP. Apparently, related keywords have been removed, and the brand name is now listed as "Johnston Law Firm" instead of "Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm." While changes will probably be made, I do not handle any modifications to GBPs; I believe Mikey takes care of that.

If the brand name for the GBP is updated to "Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm," we will also revise the text throughout the website to ensure consistency.  Have you seen anything on your end regarding Andrei's GBP note?

Moreover, with this campaign, we've lost a significant number of clicks on branded keywords, such as "Johnston Law Firm" and other variations that include "Johnston." The homepage has seen the largest decline in clicks. This issue may be attributed to the GBP problem, along with other factors as well. I believe Mubeen has prepared a more detailed report regarding this matter.

cc:

  • CRO
    • Pages complete: Contact page, Truck Page, Homepage sections, Case page.Car accident and FAQ's page
    • We are currently working on the Pop-up form when clicking on the "case evaluation button" - https://johnston-lawfirm.clixsysites.com/
    • We're currently working on optimizing  FAQ content for Case pages.
  • Blazers Partnership added to https://johnston-lawfirm.com/community/
Completed:
  • SEO
    • Update the Twitter.com Link to the X.com Link in the LegalService Schema and the Attorney Schema.
    • Updated Meta Titles and descriptions on the website
    • Crawl Check and optimized indexed pages that weren't ranking on GSC
    • Posted on the GBP
    • Posted approved content on the website.
    • Fixed internal and external 404 pages
    • MAY- optimized text on Albany, Beaverton pages to not suggest an office is present at these locations.
  • Things we are working on:
    • [High] - Directive Noindex
      [High] - XML Sitemap Over 50MB
      [High] - Directive Nofollow
      [High] - XML Sitemap With Over 50k URLs
      [High] - Images Over 100 KB
      [High] - H1 Missing
